name: QuickBooks Online

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. Access tokens expire in 1 hour, refresh tokens in 100 days. The realmId (company ID) is returned as a query parameter after authorization. Intuit introduced tiered API pricing in 2025 distinguishing Core (free) and CorePlus (metered) API calls.

api.rateLimits: 500 requests per minute per realmId. Max 10 simultaneous requests per company per app. Batch operations: 40 requests per minute per realmId. 429 status code when exceeded.

sdk.notes: node-quickbooks is a community-maintained package (npm: node-quickbooks, GitHub: mcohen01/node-quickbooks). Intuit provides official SDKs for PHP, Java, .NET, and Ruby but not an official Node.js SDK.

webhooks.registrationEndpoint: Configured in Intuit Developer Portal (dashboard), not via API
webhooks.payloadFormat: JSON POST with entity name, id, operation, lastUpdated
webhooks.notes: Webhook notifications do not count toward API rate limits. Available for both sandbox and production environments.

sandbox.notes: Free sandbox accounts with sample data available at https://developer.intuit.com. Sandbox available in multiple regions: US, UK, Canada, France, Australia, India. Uses separate base URL: sandbox-quickbooks.api.intuit.com.

CorePlus API call pricing details introduced in 2025
